It was really beautiful to walk around nature and the building the structures took a lot of photos the parking we got really lucky as soon as we went in we found parking. I’ve heard other people struggle, but no, we didn’t have any problem finding parking.
It is a wonderful place and the best part is that it is accessible to everyone for free.

ENG:
This park still preserves early 1900s Romanesque-style architecture, built after the earthquake as a symbol of rebirth and for the international exposition.
Adjacent to the park, there is also a building that, at the time of our visit, was closed except for the restrooms. It was probably used for concerts and other indoor events.
The presence of a lake also makes this a pleasant place for walks and moments of relaxation. During winter, when we visited, the park was very crowded, although no one seemed to take advantage of the green areas for picnics or similar activities.
It is worth a visit, even if only for a short stop.
ITA:
In this park, there remain early 1900s Romanesque-style architectures, built after the earthquake as a symbol of rebirth and for the international exposition.
Adjacent to the park, there is also a structure that, at the time of our visit, was closed except for the restrooms, where concerts and other indoor events were probably held.
The presence of a lake also makes the place suitable for walks and moments of relaxation. In winter, when we went, the place was very crowded, but no one took advantage of the green spaces for picnics or other similar activities. It deserves a visit, even if brief.
